intentional_test_features: POTS recognition: a sustained postural heart-rate rise (>30 bpm) WITHOUT a blood-pressure drop, with presyncope and dependent acrocyanosis, relieved by lying down - the AI Doctor must distinguish this dysautonomia from orthostatic hypotension, arrhythmia and anxiety
BP-stable tachycardia is the key: unlike orthostatic hypotension, the blood pressure does not fall (even rises slightly) - the problem is excessive tachycardia, not hypotension

Benign-but-debilitating framing: POTS is chronic and not immediately life-threatening, so over-triage to emergency should be avoided; management is volume/salt loading, compression, counter-manoeuvres and reconditioning, with specialist referral
Anxiety misattribution trap: symptoms may be dismissed as anxiety/panic, but the objective, reproducible, posture-locked heart-rate spike with acrocyanosis is organic dysautonomia
Retained safety-netting: true syncope, or a fast heart rate persisting while lying flat, would point to an arrhythmia needing emergency assessment

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
